canonical_definition: Based on the provided literature, financial question answering is defined as the behavior of answering questions within the financial domain by utilizing retrieved evidence. This task is operationalized and measured using multiple benchmarks. One paper explicitly links the behavior to the FiQA benchmark, referring to it as "Financial QA". Another source indicates that the MMLU benchmark is also used to measure financial question answering. Thus, the available data presents a consistent definition of the task, with its evaluation carried out through at least two distinct instruments.
papers: DecoupleSearch: Decouple Planning and Search via Hierarchical Reward Modeling
PBI-Attack: Prior-Guided Bimodal Interactive Black-Box Jailbreak Attack for Toxicity Maximization
SwiftKV: Fast Prefill-Optimized Inference with Knowledge-Preserving Model Transformation
